entity_relationships.business_meaning: An attendance record belongs to one employee
A baggage item is associated with one flight
A turnaround operation is performed for one flight
An employee belongs to one department
A leave request is submitted by one employee
A schedule assigns one employee to one shift
A training record belongs to one employee

business_processes.tables: attendance, department, employee, employee_role, leave_request, role, schedule, shift, training_record

use_cases.name: Flight Turnaround Management
use_cases.description: Coordinates all ground operations (catering, cleaning, refueling, boarding) for a flight's turnaround to ensure on-time departure. Tracks task completion via checklists and monitors overall turnaround status against target departure time.

use_cases.name: Baggage Tracking and Tracing
use_cases.description: Provides end-to-end visibility of baggage from check-in through loading, transit scans, unloading, and delivery. Supports lost baggage investigation and reconciliation with flight manifests.

use_cases.name: Employee Attendance and Scheduling
use_cases.description: Manages employee rosters, shift assignments, daily attendance tracking, leave requests, and training compliance. Links employees to departments and roles for operational planning.

use_cases.name: Gate and Stand Assignment
use_cases.description: Allocates gates and aircraft stands to flights based on availability and operational constraints. Tracks assignment timestamps for turnaround coordination and resource optimization.

use_cases.name: Equipment Maintenance Management
use_cases.description: Tracks equipment maintenance requests, schedules preventive maintenance, records maintenance history, and manages equipment assignments to employees. Ensures ground support equipment availability and compliance.

sample_queries.question: Find all baggage details including current status and last scan location for a specific baggage tag number
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT b.baggage_id, b.tag_number, b.status AS baggage_status, f.flight_number, f.status AS flight_status,
       bs.scan_location AS last_scan_location, bs.scan_time AS last_scan_time
FROM baggage b
JOIN flight f ON b.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT scan_location, scan_time
    FROM baggage_scan
    WHERE baggage_id = b.baggage_id
    ORDER BY scan_time DESC
    LIMIT 1
) bs ON true
WHERE b.tag_number = 'BA123456';

sample_queries.question: List all flights scheduled for today with their gate assignments, stand assignments, and turnaround operation status
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT f.flight_id, f.flight_number, f.status AS flight_status,
       ga.assigned_time AS gate_assigned_time, g.gate_number, g.status AS gate_status,
       sa.assigned_time AS stand_assigned_time, st.stand_number, st.status AS stand_status,
       to_turn.start_time AS turnaround_start, to_turn.target_departure_time, to_turn.status AS turnaround_status
FROM flight f
LEFT JOIN gate_assignment ga ON f.flight_id = ga.flight_id
LEFT JOIN gate g ON ga.gate_id = g.gate_id
LEFT JOIN stand_assignment sa ON f.flight_id = sa.flight_id
LEFT JOIN stand st ON sa.stand_id = st.stand_id
LEFT JOIN turnaround_operation to_turn ON f.flight_id = to_turn.flight_id
WHERE f.flight_id IN (
    SELECT flight_id FROM turnaround_operation 
    WHERE start_time::date = CURRENT_DATE
    UNION 
    SELECT flight_id FROM gate_assignment WHERE assigned_time::date = CURRENT_DATE
    UNION 
    SELECT flight_id FROM stand_assignment WHERE assigned_time::date = CURRENT_DATE
)
ORDER BY f.flight_number;

sample_queries.question: Analyze average turnaround time by flight and identify flights that exceeded their target departure time
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT f.flight_number, 
       to_turn.start_time, 
       to_turn.target_departure_time,
       EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (to_turn.target_departure_time - to_turn.start_time))/60 AS planned_turnaround_minutes,
       CASE 
           WHEN to_turn.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - to_turn.start_time))/60
           ELSE NULL 
       END AS actual_turnaround_minutes,
       CASE 
           WHEN to_turn.status = 'COMPLETED' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP > to_turn.target_departure_time THEN 'DELAYED'
           WHEN to_turn.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 'ON_TIME'
           ELSE 'IN_PROGRESS'
       END AS departure_performance
FROM turnaround_operation to_turn
JOIN flight f ON to_turn.flight_id = f.flight_id
WHERE to_turn.start_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'
ORDER BY to_turn.start_time DESC;

sample_queries.question: Summary of employee attendance hours by department for the current month
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT d.department_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) AS employee_count,
       SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (a.check_out - a.check_in))/3600) AS total_hours_worked,
       AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (a.check_out - a.check_in))/3600) AS avg_daily_hours,
       MIN(a.check_in) AS earliest_check_in,
       MAX(a.check_out) AS latest_check_out
FROM attendance a
JOIN employee e ON a.employee_id = e.employee_id
JOIN department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE a.attendance_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND a.attendance_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
  AND a.check_out IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name
ORDER BY total_hours_worked DESC;

sample_queries.question: Get complete turnaround task details for a specific flight including boarding, catering, cleaning, and refueling tasks with assigned employees
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT f.flight_number, to_turn.turnaround_id, to_turn.start_time, to_turn.target_departure_time, to_turn.status AS turnaround_status,
       bt.boarding_task_id, bt.gate_ready, bt.baggage_loaded, bt.status AS boarding_status,
       ct.catering_task_id, ct.catering_vendor, ct.start_time AS catering_start, ct.end_time AS catering_end, ct.status AS catering_status,
       clt.cleaning_task_id, clt.start_time AS cleaning_start, clt.end_time AS cleaning_end, clt.status AS cleaning_status,
       e.employee_name AS cleaning_employee,
       rt.refueling_task_id, rt.fuel_remaining, rt.fuel_required, rt.fuel_added, rt.status AS refueling_status
FROM turnaround_operation to_turn
JOIN flight f ON to_turn.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN boarding_task bt ON to_turn.turnaround_id = bt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN catering_task ct ON to_turn.turnaround_id = ct.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN cleaning_task clt ON to_turn.turnaround_id = clt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN employee e ON clt.assigned_employee_id = e.employee_id
LEFT JOIN refueling_task rt ON to_turn.turnaround_id = rt.turnaround_id
WHERE f.flight_number = 'AA100';
